parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877) deletes the relative/controller-centric clause (Am 98); recital 27 reworded to 'same or higher' protection, CJEU reading, independent supervisory authorities (Am 17). JURI silent (data-acquis only). Joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818) tables no Art 4(1) amendment; Kaljurand/centre-left hostile to redefinition
eesc: Must stay objective and adequately broad; broad CJEU reading
member_states: FR/PL/DE welcome deletion; PL keep Art 4(1) intact; DE wants SRB/relative-anonymity reflected; NL: keep EDPB competent on pseudonymisation guidance (Kamerbrief 18 Jun)
status: Council deletes; likely consensus to drop

parliament: No committee amendment on the GDPR research definition (JURI/IMCO data-acquis only); the joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818) does not amend the Art 4(38) definition, but keeps further-processing for research compatible while re-anchoring it to Art 89 safeguards (Am 45; recitals 29/32, Am 2-3)
eesc: Keep commonly-accepted meaning (EDPB/CJEU); enable industrial research
member_states: PL opposes deleting rec 28/def; RO wants SME innovation covered; FR wants research-independence safeguards; NL: adopt EDPB/EDPS research wording (autonomous/methodological/verifiable)

parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877) deletes Art 9(2)(k) and 9(5) (Am 100-101) and recital 33 (Am 20) — opposes the 'AI privilege' as contrary to necessity/proportionality. Stronger than Council. Joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818) tables no Art 9(2)(k)/9(5) amendment, but bars data-intermediation data being used to train AI systems (Data Act, Am 25)
member_states: FR/PL want incidental-only + 'disproportionate effort' clarified; RO wants SME-proportionate; NL: if kept, narrow to 'technically impossible' (drop 'disproportionate effort')

parliament: Joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818) tightens the derogation — 'one-to-one' verification + Union-law safeguards (Am 46; recital 34, Am 4)
member_states: FR wants Union/MS-law gate; PL restrictive + tech-neutral reading

commission: Refuse where reasonable grounds to believe other purpose
council_st9547: Retained but requires abusive intention demonstrated
parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877) deletes the abuse-of-rights restriction (Am 103-106) and adds an anti-circumvention/anti-dark-pattern duty barring abusive resort to Art 12(5) (Am 107, DMA Art 13-inspired). Joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818) reverses the abuse reframe — Art 12(5) 'choice between' a fee or refusal with the controller bearing the burden (Am 48-49; recital 35, Am 5)
eesc: Right of access is enabling; burden of proving abuse stays with controller
member_states: PL: burden on controller (welcomed) but read restrictively; FR: also catch procedure circumvention; NL: support 'abusive intention' standard (per CJEU/EDPB)

parliament: IMCO recital 44 tightens the carve-out to 'strictly necessary and proportionate' / 'explicitly requested' (Am 21); no operative Art 13 amendment. Joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818) tables no Art 13 amendment
member_states: FR supports; PL keep strictly low-risk; research derogation exceptional

council_st9547: Largely restored to current GDPR; (a) clause deleted
parliament: Joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818) restores the Art 22 right not to be subject to solely automated decisions (Am 51) and defines meaningful human intervention (Am 53; recital 38, Am 6) — goes beyond the Council
eesc: ADM must respect data-minimisation; human decision preferred where reasonable
member_states: FR backs keeping current Art 22; PL: refine not delete; EE open to COM amendment; NL: do NOT amend Art 22 (backs EDPB/EDPS/AP)

council_st9547: High-risk threshold; 96h; via national entry point
parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877): EDPB makes the breach template public and the Commission shall take utmost account of the Board (Am 108-109); new safeguard that the single entry point must not lower security/privacy/consumer/data-protection levels or limit supervisory powers (Am 110). Joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818) keeps 96h (Am 54) and has the EDPB establish/publish the template + high-risk list (Am 56-57; recital 39, Am 7)
ecb: Joint controllers should file one SEP notification (else up to 22 duplicates); add EUDPR to SEP
member_states: PL/RO: single clear 96h (flag 72h/96h ambiguity); PL keep implementing-act for template/lists

parliament: Joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818): the EDPB establishes and publishes the DPIA lists, template and methodology; Commission only 'may' adopt the template (Am 58-63; recital 40, Am 8)
member_states: PL opposes EDPB-establishes model (wants implementing act); RO wants light SME DPIA templates

council_st9547: GDPR Art 88a DELETED; consent kept in ePrivacy Art 5(3)
parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877): limits gatekeepers to one consent prompt/year per refused purpose and voids consent re-requested within the no-repeat window as an unfair practice under Dir 2005/29 (Am 115-116); opposes deleting ePrivacy Art 4 (Am 122). Joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818) does not address the cookie-consent relocation — only drafting fixes to the Art 4(32)/(33) terminal-equipment/e-comms definitions (Am 43-44)
eesc: Explore simplified consent in low-risk contexts (risk-based)
member_states: FR/PL keep cookie rules in ePrivacy (Presidency adopted); FR also wants Art 88b deleted

parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877) strengthens the signal regime: standardisation request within 3 months with balanced stakeholder representation (Am 117), extends the duty to all 'software to access online interfaces' (tech-neutral) and drops the SME exemption (Am 118), adds third-party-software + anti-dark-pattern duties (Am 119), and accelerates the browser/OS duty from 48 to 18 months (Am 120); DPA powers and fines extended to software providers (Am 112-114). Opposite to FR/industry deletion. Joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818) tables no Art 88b amendment
member_states: FR demands deletion (no impact assessment); EE: browsers should enable withdrawal

parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877) deletes Art 88c (Am 121) and recitals 30-31 (Am 18-19) — aligns with the Council deletion. EDRi/noyb push deletion; EPP more supportive of a recital. Joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818) tables no Art 88c amendment
member_states: RO: clarify/remove 'regurgitation' (rec 31); EE: don't unduly restrict legal bases; PL prior comments stand

commission: ENISA SEP; report once share many
council_st9547: Retained (national entry points + ENISA info point)
parliament: Broadly supported; IMCO adds a non-regression safeguard that the single entry point must not lower protection levels or limit supervisory powers (Am 110). Joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818) adds 'report-once' harmonised-application guidance (new NIS2 Art 14a, Am 75; recital 49a, Am 11) and a common SEP notification template (Art 23a(3a), Am 76)
ecb: Supports SEP + 'report once'; carve DORA out initially; add EUDPR to SEP
eesc: Strong support; English submissions + reuse + need-to-know access
member_states: DK/EE want MS role + later timeline review; ES direct DORA/CER/eIDAS channel; FI voluntary; AT Art 38 ambiguity

commission: Repeal and consolidate into Data Act
council_st9547: DGA/FFNPD/Open Data repealed; P2B partially amended
parliament: JURI (PE789.142, 68 amdts) and IMCO (PE789.877, 124 amdts) committee-for-opinion drafts largely reverse the deregulation: oppose the P2B repeal (JURI Am 12; IMCO Am 123-124, demanding an impact assessment), keep smart contracts/Art 36 (JURI Am 7/36), restore cloud switching (JURI Am 34), restore DGA intermediation neutrality + mandatory registration (JURI Am 13-14/37-42), restore B2G public-interest access (JURI Am 28/31); IMCO lets public bodies set higher re-use charges for gatekeepers (Am 15-16). The lead joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818) restores P2B in full (deletes the repeal, Am 77-78; repeal recital 59, Am 12) and strengthens the Data Act intermediation/B2G-emergency/third-country-access/open-data safeguards (Am 13-42)
ecb: Welcomes Data Act consolidation + FFNPD (2018/1807) repeal; supply public-interest data at cost
eesc: Limit mandatory data-sharing to strictly necessary; strengthen EDIB; favour voluntary sharing
member_states: Broad resistance to P2B repeal: {AT;ES;LV;SE} + {BE;CZ;DK;FI;IE;IT;LU;NL;SI}; BE/IT want permanent (not 2032 sunset); EE backs repeal

council_st9547: Art 41a deleted; replaced by EDPB-opinion Art 29a; Art 25 extended to processors
parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877) deletes the Commission's Art 41a implementing power (Am 111) — DPA/EDPB competence (EDPB Guidelines 01/2025), aligning with the Council. Joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818) tables no Art 29a/41a amendment
edpb_edps: Delete Art 41a; scope-defining rules belong in primary law/EDPB
eesc: Supports Commission implementing acts on pseudonymised=anonymised
member_states: FR wants binding EDPB opinion (Art 64(3)); PL opposes deleting Art 41a; RO SME-proportionate; DE reflect SRB/relative anonymity

council_st9547: EDPB establishes lists/templates + Art 32 guidance + Art 29a opinion; Art 37(7) publish DPO contact
parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877): EDPB makes templates/lists public and the Commission takes utmost account (Am 108-109); extends DPA investigative/corrective powers and fines to software/browser providers re Art 88b (Am 112-114). Joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818): Art 70 tasks have the EDPB establish (not merely propose) the DPIA lists and the DPIA/breach templates (Am 64-67)
edpb_edps: EDPB (and EDPS for EUDPR) should prepare AND approve templates/lists
member_states: FR wants controller/processor standing to ask EDPB (Art 64/65) + Art 32(5) mandate; PL prefers Commission implementing acts; NL: keep EDPB (not Commission) empowered on pseudonymisation/DPIA/breach

commission: Point-for-point mirror of GDPR Art 3 changes
council_st9547: ST 9547: not operatively amended (recital-only, alignment deferred). ST 10729/26: operative Art 4 restored mirroring the GDPR changes, EXCEPT the EUDPR Art 37 amendment is deleted (mirrors GDPR Art 88a deletion)
parliament: Joint ITRE/LIBE report (PE786.818) mirrors its GDPR changes into the EUDPR — biometric verification, Art 24 automated decisions, EDPB-led DPIA template (Am 68-74)
edpb_edps: GDPR comments apply equally; don't adopt EUDPR Art 39; EDPS approves EUI lists
ecb: Welcomes parallel GDPR/EUDPR amendment; add EUDPR to SEP

council_st9547: NEW in ST 10729/26 (22 Jun): Art 49(1)(d) replaced + (4) deleted, plus interpretive recital 40b bringing periodic/automated FATCA-style tax-data transfers to third countries under the public-interest derogation; no impact assessment
edpb_edps: Not consulted on this specific addition (JO 2/2026 predates it)
member_states: Several delegations requested it via the Antici Group (unnamed); Lehagre/AAA alleges an original Spanish push, rejected earlier
status: New & contested; to Coreper-II 26 Jun; intersects CJEU C-804/25
